engineGuide.h2: Jeep Comanche Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Comanche across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: High Output variant introduced 1991, delivering 193hp with improved cylinder head and cam profile for Comanche performance enthusiasts.
compatibleVariants: Sport 4.0L HO, Pioneer 4.0L HO, Base 4.0L HO
commonFailures: Lifter collapse causing valvetrain noise and misfires (80k+ miles)
Cylinder head cracking on 0331 casting between valves 3-4
Rear main seal oil leaks requiring engine removal
entries.cta: Get quotes for ERH Jeep Comanche engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Renix-era 4.0L engines with throttle-body injection, known for robust bottom end but sensitive ignition/electronics.
compatibleVariants: Base 4.0L Renix, Pioneer 4.0L Renix, Sport 4.0L Renix
commonFailures: Crankshaft position sensor failure causing no-start conditions
Renix ignition module overheating and intermittent misfires
Closed cooling system overheating leading to head gasket failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for Renix 4.0L Jeep Comanche engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Standard output 4.0L engine used across base Comanche models, sharing architecture with XJ Cherokee for parts compatibility.
compatibleVariants: Base 4.0L, Pioneer 4.0L, Sport 4.0L, Long Bed 4.0L, Short Bed 4.0L
commonFailures: Cylinder head cracking on high-mileage units with poor cooling maintenance
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling on cold starts
Intake manifold gasket leaks leading to rough idle and vacuum issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for EPE Jeep Comanche engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Base engine for entry-level Comanche models, valued for fuel economy but prone to Renix-era electronic ignition issues.
commonFailures: Crankshaft position sensor failure causing intermittent no-start (Renix era)
Throttle body injection carbon buildup leading to hesitation and stalling
Timing chain stretch on neglected high-mileage units causing timing drift
entries.cta: Get quotes for EPE 2.5L Jeep Comanche engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Petrol
entries.familyHeading: Specialist & Import Engine Codes (1986 Only / European Market)
entries.history: One-year-only GM-sourced 60-degree V6 for 1986 Comanche, rare and sought by collectors restoring original-spec vehicles.
commonFailures: Intake manifold gasket failure causing coolant leaks and overheating
Cylinder head warping under sustained load due to thin casting design
Oil pump wear leading to low oil pressure warnings at idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for GM2.8V6 Jeep Comanche engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.familyHeading: Specialist & Import Engine Codes (1986 Only / European Market)
entries.history: Renault-sourced turbo diesel for European-market Comanche imports, prized for torque but limited UK parts availability.
compatibleVariants: 2.1L Diesel European Import, 2.1L Diesel LHD UK Import
commonFailures: Injection pump wear causing hard starting and loss of power under load
Turbocharger seal failure leading to oil consumption and blue exhaust smoke
Head gasket failure on overheated units causing coolant/oil contamination

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Comanche Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Comanche's 4.0L straight-six and 2.5L four-cylinder engines are generally robust, but age-related failures like lifter collapse, CPS sensor issues, and cooling system problems drive replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Jeep Comanche 4.0L Lifter Failure - Why Valvetrain Noise Leads to Engine Replacement
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Comanche 4.0L Renix & HO (1987-1992, ERH, HAM4.0T5LYX6, EPE)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic lifter collapse due to oil starvation or wear, causing ticking noise, misfires, and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, inspect lifters, adjust rockers, replace faulty lifters only
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; only viable if caught early before valve damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace all lifters, resurface head, new head gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; suitable if block is undamaged and mileage <100k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded lifters, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines; eliminates recurring valvetrain issues

problems.recommendation: On a Comanche worth £3,000-£8,000, if the block is sound and mileage is under 100k, a cylinder head service offers good value. For engines over 120k miles or with known block issues, a rebuilt long block is the more reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4.0L ERH engine prices for your Jeep Comanche

problems.group: Jeep Comanche Crankshaft Position Sensor Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Comanche Crankshaft Position Sensor Failure - The Renix No-Start Crisis
problems.rootCause: CPS sensor overheating or internal coil failure interrupts spark signal, causing intermittent no-start or sudden stalling while driving.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crankshaft position sensor, clear codes, test ignition system
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix; new OEM-spec sensor typically lasts 40k-60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace CPS, ignition coil, module, and wiring harness connectors
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses systemic Renix ignition weaknesses; reduces recurrence risk

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Updated Ignition
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with modernized ignition components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with multiple Renix-era failures; future-proofs the powertrain

problems.recommendation: Start with CPS replacement-it's the most common failure point. If no-start persists after sensor replacement, investigate the ignition module and wiring before considering engine replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renix 4.0L Jeep Comanche engine replacement

problems.h4: Jeep Comanche 4.0L Cylinder Head Cracking - The 0331 Casting Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Comanche 4.0L All Variants (1987-2001 compatible, ERH, EPE, AMC4.0)
problems.rootCause: 0331 cylinder head casting develops cracks between exhaust valves 3 and 4 due to thermal cycling and inadequate cooling maintenance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, weld crack, pressure test, resurface, reinstall with new gasket
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for minor cracks; success depends on crack location and welder skill

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Source good-condition replacement head (0630/7120 casting), install with new gasket and hardware
repairOptions.longevity: More reliable than welding; ensures known-good casting is fitted

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Head
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded cylinder head casting, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; eliminates recurrence risk and addresses other age-related wear

problems.recommendation: If the block is in good condition and mileage is moderate, a replacement head (0630 or 7120 casting) offers the best balance of cost and reliability. For high-mileage engines, a full rebuilt unit is more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4.0L cylinder head Jeep Comanche engine prices

problems.group: Jeep Comanche Closed Cooling System Overheating
problems.h4: Jeep Comanche Closed Cooling System Overheating - Head Gasket Failure Risk
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Comanche 4.0L & 2.5L (1987-1990 Renix-era, closed cooling system variants)
problems.rootCause: Air pockets trapped in closed cooling system cause localized overheating, leading to head gasket failure and potential head warping.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Flush system, replace thermostat, bleed air pockets, pressure test for leaks
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative only; not suitable if head gasket is already compromised

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace gasket, resurface head/block, reinstall with new hardware
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom; viable if head is not warped and block is undamaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Improved Cooling
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded cooling components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with recurrent overheating; includes improved water pump and thermostat

problems.recommendation: If overheating is caught early and the head is not warped, a head gasket replacement with proper cooling system bleeding can restore reliability. For engines with known warping or recurrent issues, a rebuilt unit is the safer investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for overheating-repaired Jeep Comanche 4.0L engine

problems.group: Jeep Comanche Rear Main Seal Leak
problems.h4: Jeep Comanche Rear Main Seal Leak - Oil Loss Without Obvious Source
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Comanche 4.0L All Variants (1986-1992, ERH, EPE, AMC4.0)
problems.rootCause: Rear main seal hardens and shrinks with age, allowing oil to seep from crankshaft rear flange into bellhousing.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Add rear main seal conditioner to oil; may slow leak but does not repair
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term mitigation only; not a repair solution

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove transmission, replace rear main seal, reinstall with new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses leak directly; labour-intensive but cost-effective if engine is otherwise sound

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with New Seals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with all new seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other age-related wear; eliminates multiple potential leak points

problems.recommendation: If the engine is otherwise healthy and mileage is moderate, seal replacement is cost-effective. For high-mileage engines or those with additional leaks, a rebuilt unit offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Comanche 4.0L engine with new seals

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Comanche
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing lifter noise, a no-start CPS failure, or coolant loss from a cracked head, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Comanche specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus nationwide delivery and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your classic Comanche back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Comanche Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Comanche and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

fuelTypes.h2: Jeep Comanche Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Jeep Comanche engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from the economical 2.5L petrol to the rare 2.1L diesel import.

items.title: Jeep Comanche Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines dominated Comanche production, with the 4.0L straight-six accounting for most replacement enquiries due to its popularity and known failure patterns like lifter collapse and head cracking.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines dominated Comanche production, with the 4.0L straight-six accounting for most replacement enquiries due to its popularity and known failure patterns like lifter collapse and head cracking.
items.familiesLabel: Common Jeep Comanche Petrol Engine Families
families: ERH - 4.0L HO (1991-1992), most sought-after variant for performance restorations
HAM4.0T5LYX6 - Renix 4.0L (1987-1990), known for CPS sensor and ignition module issues
EPE/AMC2.5 - 2.5L four-cylinder (1986-1992), budget option with TBI reliability concerns
GM2.8V6 - Rare 1986-only V6, collector-focused with limited parts availability
foundIn: First Generation MJ (1986-1992) - All petrol variants listed above
knownFor: Robust AMC 4.0L architecture shared with XJ Cherokee for cross-compatibility
Renix-era ignition system sensitivity requiring specialist diagnostic knowledge
Cylinder head cracking on 0331 casting under sustained high-temperature operation
Strong DIY community support for maintenance and replacement guidance

engineYears.h2: Jeep Comanche Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Comanche's 1986-1992 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Year: 2.5L Base & Rare 2.8L V6 Debut
years.description: The Comanche launched with two petrol options: the economical 2.5L four-cylinder and the one-year-only 2.8L GM V6, setting the foundation for MJ engine compatibility.
keyChanges: First production year for Jeep Comanche (MJ platform)
Introduction of 2.5L AMC four-cylinder and 2.8L GM V6 engine options
Shared architecture with XJ Cherokee established for parts interchangeability
mainEngines: GM2.8V6/LR2 - 2.8L V6 petrol (1986 only, all trims)
knownFor: 2.8L V6 rare and sought by collectors restoring original-spec vehicles
Early Renix electronics on 2.5L prone to sensor-related no-start issues
Strong chassis and drivetrain foundation shared with XJ Cherokee
years.cta: Get quotes for 1986 Jeep Comanche replacement engines
years.ticker: 1986 Comanche 2.8L V6 engine • 2.5L Renix no-start fix • XJ donor engine compatibility

years.description: The legendary 4.0L AMC straight-six arrived with Renix throttle-body injection, becoming the Comanche's most popular engine despite ignition system sensitivities.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4.0L AMC straight-six engine across Comanche range
Closed cooling system design introduced, later linked to overheating concerns
knownFor: 4.0L Renix praised for torque and durability but sensitive to CPS and ignition module failures
Closed cooling system prone to air pockets causing localized overheating
Strong aftermarket support for Renix diagnostics and upgrades
years.cta: Get quotes for 1987-1990 Jeep Comanche Renix engine replacement
years.ticker: Renix 4.0L CPS failure • Comanche overheating fix • 4.0L lifter tick repair

years.preview: High Output Era: 4.0L HO Performance Peak
years.description: The final Comanche years introduced the High Output 4.0L with improved cylinder head and cam profile, delivering 193hp and becoming the most desirable variant for restorations.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4.0L High Output (HO) engine with 193hp output
Updated cylinder head casting (0630/7120) addressing earlier cracking concerns
Final production years for Comanche before discontinuation in 1992
mainEngines: EPE/AMC4.0 - 4.0L standard petrol (173hp, continued for base models)
knownFor: 4.0L HO considered the pinnacle of Comanche performance and collectibility
Improved head casting reduced but did not eliminate cracking risk under neglect
Strong residual value driving investment in quality rebuilt engines for restoration
years.cta: Get quotes for 1991-1992 Jeep Comanche HO engine replacement
years.ticker: 4.0L HO engine for sale • Comanche 1992 last year engine • HO vs Renix compatibility

faq.h2: Jeep Comanche Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Will a Jeep Cherokee engine fit a Comanche?
items.answer: Yes, the Comanche shares its engine architecture with the XJ Cherokee (1984-2001), making most 4.0L and 2.5L engines directly compatible with proper mounting hardware and ECU matching.
keyPoints: 4.0L straight-six engines are fully interchangeable between Comanche and XJ Cherokee
Verify engine code (ERH, EPE, Renix) matches your Comanche year and electronics
Mounting points, transmission bellhousing and accessory brackets align across platforms
ECU and wiring harness may require adaptation for Renix vs HO variants
items.cta: Compare XJ-compatible 4.0L engine prices for your Jeep Comanche

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Jeep Comanche engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt engine costs range from £950 for a 2.5L four-cylinder to £3,200 for a 4.0L High Output, with supply-only pricing; fitting labour adds £450-£850 if using a specialist.
keyPoints: 2.5L rebuilt engines: £950-£2,100 supply only
4.0L Renix rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800; HO variant: £1,450-£3,200
Rare engines (2.8L V6, 2.1L diesel): £1,800-£4,500 due to scarcity
All EngineMarket suppliers include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get a tailored Jeep Comanche engine replacement quote today

items.question: What is the difference between Renix and High Output 4.0L engines?
items.answer: Renix (1987-1990) uses throttle-body injection with 173hp; High Output (1991-1992) features improved cylinder head, cam profile and 193hp output with updated engine management.
keyPoints: Renix: 173hp, throttle-body injection, prone to CPS and ignition module failures
HO: 193hp, multi-port injection, improved 0630/7120 cylinder head casting
ECU, wiring harness and intake manifold differ between the two systems
HO engines command higher replacement value due to performance and rarity
items.cta: Compare Renix vs HO 4.0L engine prices for your Comanche

items.question: Is the Jeep Comanche 4.0L engine reliable?
items.answer: The 4.0L AMC straight-six is fundamentally robust, but age-related failures like lifter collapse, cylinder head cracking and CPS sensor issues drive replacement demand on high-mileage examples.
keyPoints: Bottom end is exceptionally durable; failures typically stem from ancillary components
Regular cooling system maintenance prevents head gasket and cracking issues
Upgraded lifters and improved head castings in rebuilt units address known weaknesses
Strong DIY community and parts availability support long-term ownership
items.cta: Find a rebuilt 4.0L Comanche engine with upgraded components

items.question: What causes the Jeep Comanche no-start issue on Renix models?
items.answer: Crankshaft position sensor failure is the most common cause, interrupting the spark signal; ignition module overheating and wiring harness degradation are secondary culprits.
keyPoints: CPS sensor located on bellhousing; fails due to heat and vibration over time
Symptoms: engine cranks but no spark, intermittent stalling while driving
Replacement sensor costs £40-£80; labour 1-2 hours for DIY-capable owners
Upgraded ignition components in rebuilt engines reduce recurrence risk
items.cta: Get quotes for Renix ignition-repaired Comanche engine replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a classic Comanche?
items.answer: For restorable Comanches valued £3,000-£8,000, a rebuilt engine often makes financial sense versus parting out, especially when using XJ Cherokee donor compatibility to control costs.
keyPoints: Comanche values rising among classic truck enthusiasts and off-road collectors
Rebuilt 4.0L engines (£1,450-£3,200) preserve vehicle value and usability
XJ Cherokee donor engines offer cost-effective replacement with proven compatibility
EngineMarket warranty protection reduces long-term ownership risk
items.cta: Compare rebuilt engine prices to decide on your Comanche restoration

items.question: What engines are compatible with the Comanche MJ for swaps?
items.answer: Beyond direct 4.0L/2.5L replacements, popular swap options include the 4.0L HO upgrade, 5.2L/5.9L V8 (with adapter kits), and modern LS conversions for performance builds.
keyPoints: 4.0L HO is a bolt-in upgrade for Renix Comanches with ECU adaptation
V8 swaps require transmission, crossmember and wiring modifications
LS conversions offer modern reliability but need significant fabrication
Always verify emissions and MOT compliance for modified engines in the UK
items.cta: Enquire about performance engine options for your Jeep Comanche

items.question: How long does a Comanche engine replacement take for DIY owners?
items.answer: Experienced DIY mechanics typically complete a Comanche engine swap in 2-4 days with proper tools; supply-only engines allow flexible scheduling without specialist booking delays.
keyPoints: Comanche's body-on-frame design simplifies engine removal vs unibody vehicles
Allow extra time for Renix wiring harness adaptation or HO conversion steps
EngineMarket suppliers can coordinate delivery timing to match your project schedule
Post-installation tuning and break-in procedures critical for longevity
items.cta: Find supply-only Jeep Comanche engines for your DIY project

items.question: What warranty coverage comes with replacement Comanche engines?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects, with upgrade options available.
keyPoints: Warranty covers rebuilt, reconditioned and remanufactured units from vetted UK specialists
Supply & fit installations include labour coverage; supply-only covers engine components
Keep service records and use recommended break-in procedures to maintain coverage
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before order confirmation
items.cta: Compare warranty terms across Jeep Comanche engine suppliers

items.question: Can I upgrade from a Renix 4.0L to a High Output engine in my Comanche?
items.answer: Yes, the HO 4.0L is a popular upgrade for Renix Comanches, requiring ECU, wiring harness and intake manifold changes but delivering 20hp gain and improved reliability.
keyPoints: HO cylinder head and cam profile bolt directly to Renix block with adapter gaskets
Source complete HO donor engine with matching ECU and harness for simplified swap
Tuning may be needed to optimise performance after conversion
Upgraded components in rebuilt HO units address known Renix-era weaknesses
items.cta: Get quotes for HO upgrade engine packages for your Jeep Comanche
